title: Migrate changelog config to object-only format
body:
Normalize the changelog config field from polymorphic (boolean | object) to object-only format.

## Context

Currently `changelog` accepts three forms:
- `true` / omitted — enabled with defaults
- `false` — disabled
- `{"file": "CHANGES.md", "ignore_prefixes": ["chore:"]}` — enabled with overrides

This polymorphism prevents the vendor config schema from declaring a single type for the field. Migrating to object-only (`{"enabled": true, "file": "CHANGELOG.md", "ignore_prefixes": []}`) makes the config surface unambiguous and schema-typeable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `_parse_changelog_value()` and `parse_changelog_config()` accept the new object format with `enabled` key
- [ ] Boolean values (`true`/`false`) are still accepted for backward compatibility, coerced internally to the object form
- [ ] `templates/semver/config.json` updated to use the new object format as the default
- [ ] README updated to document the new canonical format (and note boolean shorthand is deprecated)
- [ ] Existing tests updated / new tests added covering: object form with `enabled: true`, object form with `enabled: false`, boolean `true` (backward compat), boolean `false` (backward compat), object with all sub-keys (`enabled`, `file`, `ignore_prefixes`)
- [ ] No behavioral change for existing configs — all current valid configs continue to work

title: Create templates/config.schema for git-semver
body:
Add a config schema file declaring all config fields that git-semver owns, for use by the git-vendored validation framework.

## Context

The git-vendored framework installs vendor schemas to `.vendored/manifests/<vendor>.schema` in consumer repos. The `audit` command validates consumer configs against these schemas (required fields, type checking, unknown field detection, cross-vendor misplacement).

## Schema fields to declare

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `version_file` | string | false | Path to file storing the current version (default: VERSION) |
| `files` | array | false | Glob patterns for files whose changes trigger automatic patch bumps |
| `updates` | object | false | File path → update action mapping for version bumps |
| `changelog` | object | false | Changelog generation config (enabled, file, ignore_prefixes) |
| `install` | object | false | Installed workflow behavior (on_merge, automerge) |

Note: Subdirectory keys (monorepo support) are dynamic user-defined names and are NOT declared in the schema. The `_vendor` key is framework-managed and excluded by the audit tool.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `templates/config.schema` exists and follows the schema format: `{"fields": {"name": {"type": "...", "required": bool, "description": "..."}}}`
- [ ] All five git-semver-owned fields are declared with correct types
- [ ] No fields belonging to other vendors or the framework are included
- [ ] `changelog` is typed as `object` (depends on gsv-356b.1 completing the migration)
- [ ] README documents the schema file and its purpose
- [ ] install.sh updated to include the schema in the manifest (if not already handled by glob)
